code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of airport‑related triples into a fluent single sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Templates for predicates – they assume the subject will be added later
    frag_templates = {
        "cityServed": "serves {object}",
        "location": "is located in {object}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "lies {object} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {object}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {object} metres",
        "runwayName": "has runway {object}",
        "country": "is in {object}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {object}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway {object} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway {object} metres long",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has runway number {object}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "runway surface is {object}",
        # added templates for better natural language
        "language": "language is {object}",
        "leader": "leader is {object}",
        "capital": "capital is {object}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language is {object}",
        "regionServed": "serves region {object}",
        "leaderParty": "leader party is {object}",
        "leaderTitle": "leader title is {object}",
        "owner": "owner is {object}",
        "jurisdiction": "jurisdiction is {object}",
        "postalCode": "postal code is {object}",
        "areaCode": "area code is {object}",
        "foundingYear": "founded in {object}",
        "founder": "founded by {object}",
        "headquarter": "headquartered at {object}",
        "nativeName": "native name is {object}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "IATA code is {object}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "ICAO code is {object}",
        "locationIdentifier": "location identifier is {object}",
        # generic fallback
    }

    # Helper: turn a predicate/object into a readable fragment
    def make_fragment(triple: Triple) -> str:
        tmpl = frag_templates.get(triple.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            return tmpl.format(object=triple.object)
        # generic fallback – make predicate readable
        pred = triple.predicate.replace("_", " ")
        return f"{pred} {triple.object}"

    # Build a mapping from subject to its triples
    subj_to_triples: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        subj_to_triples.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    # Keep track of subjects that have already been embedded as a relative clause
    embedded: set[str] = set()

    # Recursive description generator for a given subject
    def describe(subject: str, visited: set[str]) -> str:
        """Return a fragment list for *subject* (without the subject itself)."""
        if subject in visited:
            return ""  # avoid cycles
        visited = visited | {subject}
        frags = [make_fragment(t) for t in subj_to_triples.get(subject, [])]
        # Join fragments with commas and an 'and' before the last one
        if not frags:
            return ""
        if len(frags) == 1:
            return frags[0]
        return ", ".join(frags[:-1]) + ", and " + frags[-1]

    # Build the main sentence
    main_clauses: list[str] = []
    for subj, triples_list in subj_to_triples.items():
        if subj in embedded:
            continue

        # Create base fragments for this subject
        base_frags = []
        relative_clauses = []

        for t in triples_list:
            # If the object is another known subject, treat it as a relative clause
            if t.object in subj_to_triples and t.object != subj:
                # generate description for the object (excluding back‑reference)
                rel_desc = describe(t.object, {subj})
                if rel_desc:
                    # embed as a relative clause after the predicate fragment
                    pred_frag = make_fragment(t)
                    base_frags.append(f"{pred_frag}, which {rel_desc}")
                    embedded.add(t.object)
                    continue
            # regular fragment
            base_frags.append(make_fragment(t))

        # Combine base fragments
        if not base_frags:
            continue
        if len(base_frags) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {base_frags[0]}"
        else:
            clause = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(base_frags[:-1]) + ", and " + base_frags[-1]

        main_clauses.append(clause)

    # Join all main clauses with commas and finish with a period
    sentence = ", ".join(main_clauses) + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
